comment: Quality Gates catalog -- security dimension. v0.12.0 ships stub rows only (NOT-VERIFIED + waiver until 2026-07-26 tracked_in v0.12.1 SEC-* placeholders). v0.12.1 MIGRATE-03 ships the verifier scripts + flips status to PASS. Schema spec: quality/catalogs/README.md. CLAUDE.md threat-model section drives the contract: outbound HTTP allowlist + audit-log immutability are the two P0 surfaces this dimension defends.

sources: CLAUDE.md threat-model section -- Outbound HTTP allowlist

asserts: v0.12.1 stub -- a fuzz test attempts outbound HTTP to an origin not in REPOSIX_ALLOWED_ORIGINS and the http::client() factory rejects with the documented error
the disallowed_methods clippy lint catches direct reqwest::Client::new outside core/http.rs (already covered by code/clippy-lint-loaded; this row is the runtime allowlist test)

rows.owner_hint: WAIVED→PASS 2026-07-05 -- gate executed green locally (real cargo run) + confirmed in P92 CI run 28735908764 (git 2.54, all jobs success). `cargo test -p reposix-core --test http_allowlist` ran 12 tests (13 fns, 1 #[ignore]d timeout case) all passing, plus both static greps (clippy.toml disallowed-methods + load_allowlist_from_env-before-send ordering) confirmed true. See quality/reports/verifications/security/allowlist-enforcement.json for the full run artifact. Prior P90 90-05 (2026-07-04) history: quality/gates/security/allowlist-enforcement.sh landed as a wrapper around a pre-existing real test file but had not yet been executed via a real cargo run under the build-memory-budget constraint -- this run closes that gap.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the allowlist gate actually failed to reject a non-allowlisted origin, `cargo test -p reposix-core --test http_allowlist` would report a FAILED test (e.g. egress_to_non_allowlisted_host_is_rejected) instead of Error::InvalidOrigin, and the wrapper script's asserts_failed array would name it directly.

sources: CLAUDE.md threat-model section -- Audit log is append-only
rows.command: cargo test -p reposix-core --test audit_schema && cargo test -p reposix-cache --test audit_is_append_only

asserts: v0.12.1 stub -- a test attempts UPDATE/DELETE against either audit table and the SQLite layer rejects (append-only by schema)
both tables present in the test fixture

rows.owner_hint: WAIVED→PASS 2026-07-05 -- gate executed green locally (real cargo run) + confirmed in P92 CI run 28735908764 (git 2.54, all jobs success). `cargo test -p reposix-core --test audit_schema` ran 8/8 passing (append-only triggers reject UPDATE/DELETE, H-02 hardening) AND `cargo test -p reposix-cache --test audit_is_append_only` ran 1/1 passing, plus the WAL-mode grep on the cache-side connection confirmed true. See quality/reports/verifications/security/audit-immutability.json for the full run artifact. NOTE asymmetry (unchanged, tracked separately): reposix-core::audit::open_audit_db does NOT set WAL explicitly -- only the cache-side connection is WAL-tuned; the script only ever claimed WAL where it's real, so this is a durability-mode difference, not an immutability hole (immutability is enforced by the append-only triggers, covered by the tests above). Prior P90 90-05 (2026-07-04) history: quality/gates/security/audit-immutability.sh landed as a wrapper around pre-existing real test files but had not yet been executed via a real cargo run under the build-memory-budget constraint -- this run closes that gap.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If either audit table actually accepted an UPDATE/DELETE, `audit_schema.rs`'s or `audit_is_append_only.rs`'s trigger-rejection assertion would fail to see an 'append-only' error and the row survival count would drop -- a real schema regression is the falsifying evidence, not an inferred one.

rows.comment: Stage-2 catalog-row minting (2026-07-04). Substrate already exists (commit a0c84a3): connector_audit_wired_confluence / connector_audit_wired_jira tests in reposix-remote's backend_dispatch.rs.

asserts: cargo test -p reposix-remote connector_audit_wired exits 0 and runs >=2 tests
a static grep of crates/reposix-remote/src/backend_dispatch.rs confirms build_confluence and build_jira (the non-test production dispatch paths) each call .with_audit(...) before returning

rows.owner_hint: if RED: build_confluence or build_jira in crates/reposix-remote/src/backend_dispatch.rs stopped calling .with_audit(...) before returning the connector, or the connector_audit_wired_* tests regressed -- OP-3 (CLAUDE.md) requires every dispatched write-capable connector to have audit_events wired
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs `cargo test -p reposix-remote connector_audit_wired` (>=2 tests, each building a real Confluence/JIRA connector via the production build_confluence/build_jira functions and asserting backend.has_audit() plus a real audit_events table row) AND greps backend_dispatch.rs to confirm build_confluence/build_jira (outside #[cfg(test)]) call .with_audit(...). If the claim ('production dispatch wires audit for write-capable real connectors') were false -- e.g. someone removed .with_audit(audit) from build_jira -- the cargo test would fail its has_audit() assertion OR the grep would find zero with_audit call sites in the production functions, and the verifier would exit non-zero.

rows.comment: P107 (2026-07-12) catalog-first mint. Local/catalogued counterpart to the CI cargo-audit job (.github/workflows/audit.yml). Encodes the RUSTSEC advisory posture as a GREEN contract: 0 live advisories + both v0.14.0-era advisories (RUSTSEC-2026-0185 quinn-proto, RUSTSEC-2026-0186 memmap2) confirmed cleared by version floor. Ground-truth evidence: .planning/milestones/v0.14.0-phases/evidence/p107-cargo-audit-2026-07-12.txt.
sources: SECURITY.md -- Advisory posture (cargo audit / RUSTSEC) section

asserts: cargo audit exits 0 with 0 live vulnerabilities across the workspace Cargo.lock
RUSTSEC-2026-0185 (quinn-proto) confirmed CLEARED: installed quinn-proto >= 0.11.15 patched floor met (transitive-and-absent -- orphan Cargo.lock entry, never built)
RUSTSEC-2026-0186 (memmap2) confirmed CLEARED: installed memmap2 >= 0.9.11 patched floor met (transitive via gix 0.83.0; advisory is informational=unsound, gated only by -D unsound)
a committed RUSTSEC posture doc (SECURITY.md -- Advisory posture section) names both RUSTSEC-2026-0185 and RUSTSEC-2026-0186 with an honest reachability verdict

rows.owner_hint: if RED: (a) cargo audit reported a NEW live advisory -- read the named RUSTSEC id and bump the affected crate's floor via a dependabot/cargo-update PR; (b) memmap2 dropped below 0.9.11 or quinn-proto below 0.11.15 -- a lockfile regen pulled an unpatched floor; (c) SECURITY.md lost its Advisory posture section naming both advisory ids. Network-flaky cargo-audit (advisory-db fetch failure) is distinguished in the artifact via a fetch-failure marker, NOT counted as a live advisory. CI counterpart: .github/workflows/audit.yml.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The claim is 'reposix has 0 live RUSTSEC advisories and both RUSTSEC-2026-0185/0186 are cleared by version floor, not merely unreported.' The verifier runs the real `cargo audit` (exit non-zero => live advisory => FAIL) AND independently parses the installed memmap2 version from the dependency graph asserting >= 0.9.11, AND greps SECURITY.md for both advisory ids. If the claim were false -- e.g. a lockfile regen pulled memmap2 0.9.10 (unpatched), or a new advisory landed -- cargo audit would exit non-zero OR the version-floor assert would fail OR the posture-doc grep would miss an id, and the verifier would exit non-zero naming the failing assert. A green cargo audit alone cannot mask a floor regression because the floor is checked separately from the audit exit code.
